title: Licensing regime for virtual asset trading platforms comes into effect
summary: Centralised virtual asset trading platforms operating in Hong Kong, or actively marketing to Hong Kong investors, must be licensed and regulated by the Securities and Futures Commission. Two parallel licensing routes apply: a Securities and Futures Ordinance licence, covering Type 1 (dealing in securities) and Type 7 (automated trading services) regulated activities, for platforms trading security tokens; and an Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorist Financing Ordinance licence, covering the operation of a virtual asset exchange, for platforms trading non-security tokens. Because a token's legal classification can shift between non-security and security status over time, the SFC states it would be appropriate for platforms to apply for licences under both regimes, to avoid inadvertently breaching either one and to maintain business continuity.
why_it_matters: This is the legal foundation for every licensed virtual asset exchange serving Hong Kong investors, and explains why a single platform may need two separate licences rather than one.

citations.quote: are required to be licensed and regulated by the SFC
